note: Curated EU GDPR topic crosswalk for retrieval boost. Each topic ties a common privacy-law question to the controlling article(s) and supporting recital(s) or Chapter V provisions.

topics.title: Personal Data Breach Notification and Communication
topics.summary: GDPR's two breach-response duties: notification to the supervisory authority within 72 hours of awareness (Art. 33) and communication to data subjects without undue delay when high-risk (Art. 34). Includes content requirements and the high-risk carve-outs.
answer_cautions: 72-hour rule is for SA only; subject-facing communication (Art. 34) is 'without undue delay' and gated by high-risk.
Art. 34(3) carve-outs (rendered unintelligible / risk eliminated / disproportionate effort) replace individual communication, not eliminate the SA notification.

topics.title: Lawful Basis for Processing and Conditions for Consent
topics.summary: GDPR Art. 6 lawful bases (consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, legitimate interests) and the strict consent conditions of Art. 7 (and Art. 8 for children).
answer_cautions: Recitals are interpretive aids, not binding. Cite the underlying Article for binding rules.
Legitimate-interest balancing (6(1)(f)) requires a documented assessment; cannot be the default for direct marketing across all member states.

topics.title: Data Subject Rights (Access / Rectification / Erasure / Restriction / Portability / Objection)
topics.summary: Chapter III rights: access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), objection (Art. 21).

primary_authorities.label: GDPR Art. 17 (erasure / right to be forgotten)

answer_cautions: Each right has different exemptions and grounds — do not flatten into one generic 'data subject right'.
One-month default response window (Art. 12(3)), extendable by two months for complex requests.

topics.title: Special Categories of Personal Data (Sensitive Data)
topics.summary: GDPR Art. 9 prohibition on processing special categories (health, biometric, genetic, racial/ethnic, political, religious, trade-union, sex life/orientation) and the ten exhaustive derogations.
answer_cautions: Art. 9 derogations are exhaustive — member-state law may add restrictions but cannot expand grounds.
Explicit consent under Art. 9(2)(a) is stricter than Art. 6 consent; document the explicit nature.

topics.title: International Data Transfers (Chapter V)
topics.summary: Transfer of personal data to third countries: adequacy decisions (Art. 45), appropriate safeguards including SCCs and BCRs (Art. 46-47), and derogations for specific situations (Art. 49). Built on the Art. 44 general principle.
answer_cautions: Schrems II (C-311/18) requires a transfer impact assessment even when SCCs are used; mention but do not over-claim.
Art. 49 derogations are narrowly construed and not for routine transfers.

topics.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment and Prior Consultation
topics.summary: When a DPIA is required (Art. 35) and the obligation to consult the supervisory authority before processing where residual high risk remains (Art. 36).
answer_cautions: Each member state's SA may publish a 'must DPIA' / 'never DPIA' list — local SA guidance overrides generic mapping.
DPIA is a process, not a one-off form; review when processing changes materially.

topics.title: Automated Individual Decision-Making, Including Profiling
topics.summary: GDPR Art. 22 right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing (including profiling) producing legal or similarly significant effects, with the three statutory exceptions.
answer_cautions: Art. 22 applies to decisions based 'solely' on automated processing — human-in-the-loop changes the analysis.
Three statutory exceptions (contract necessity / EU or member-state law / explicit consent) are exhaustive.

topics.title: Enforcement, Remedies, Liability, and Penalties
topics.summary: Right to lodge a complaint (Art. 77), right to compensation (Art. 82), and the two-tier administrative fines structure of Art. 83 (up to 10/20 million EUR or 2/4% of global turnover).

supporting_authorities.label: GDPR Art. 77 (right to lodge a complaint)

answer_cautions: Art. 83 has TWO fine tiers — distinguish 10M/2% (lower-tier infringements) from 20M/4% (upper-tier infringements).
Compensation under Art. 82 is judicial; administrative fines under Art. 83 are imposed by the SA.
